summary: During tooth regeneration, newly formed cementum consists mainly of acellular material due to limited cell activity; instead, it focuses on producing an organized mineralized matrix essential for attachment stability and mechanical support functions specific to periodontal ligament interactions post-repair or growth phases observed clinically within affected teeth structures undergoing
